model_answer: Overfitting happens when a model learns the training data too closely, including noise, so it performs poorly on new data.
reference_answer: Overfitting means a model fits the training data too closely and does not generalize well to unseen data.

criteria.name: correctness
criteria.description: Does the answer explain the concept accurately?

criteria.name: clarity
criteria.description: Is the answer easy to understand?

criteria.name: completeness
criteria.description: Does the answer mention generalization to unseen data?

task: Explain gradient descent in exactly two sentences for a beginner.
model_answer: Gradient descent is a way for a model to improve by checking which direction makes its mistakes smaller. It repeats small updates until the model gets closer to a good answer.
reference_answer: Gradient descent is an optimization method that updates model parameters in the direction that reduces error.

criteria.name: constraint_adherence
criteria.description: Does the answer use exactly two sentences?

criteria.name: beginner_clarity
criteria.description: Is the explanation understandable for a beginner?

criteria.name: accuracy
criteria.description: Does the answer preserve the core idea of gradient descent?

model_answer: FastAPI is a Python framework for building web APIs. It helps developers define routes, validate data, and generate interactive documentation automatically.
reference_answer: FastAPI is a modern Python web framework for building APIs with type hints, validation, and automatic docs.
